claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children you have
- Their names and ages

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Alex is 8 years old, so within the age range for Child Benefit.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Do you live in the UK?** (Or are you a Crown servant or member of HM Forces posted overseas?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship and responsibility for Alex:

**Are you Alex's parent, or do you have parental responsibility for Alex?** 

(This could include being a parent, adoptive parent, guardian, or having a residence/child arrangements order)

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now an important question about where Alex lives:

**Does Alex live with you?** If not, or if Alex lives with someone else as well, please let me know the living arrangements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to check a few more details:

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?**

(At 8 years old, Alex would typically be in primary school, but I just need to confirm Alex is in education)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital or in local authority care for more than 12 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or in local authority care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) subject to immigration control, or do you have any immigration restrictions that prevent you from claiming public funds?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any immigration restrictions.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the key eligibility criteria:

✅ You are Alex's parent with parental responsibility  
✅ You live in the UK  
✅ Alex (age 8) lives with you  
✅ Alex is under 16 and in full-time education  
✅ Alex is not in extended hospital or care placement  
✅ You have no immigration restrictions preventing you from claiming public funds  

**Next steps:** You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. You should apply as soon as possible, as your claim can only be backdated for up to 3 months.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £50,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge, though you can still claim Child Benefit. You can choose to opt out of receiving payments but still register for Child Benefit to protect your National Insurance credits.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16 and within the eligible age range for Child Benefit. The claimant is Alex's parent and has parental responsibility. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK. Alex is in full-time education (primary school), which is required for children. Alex is not in hospital or local authority care for an extended period. The claimant has no immigration restrictions preventing them from claiming public funds. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. All key eligibility criteria are satisfied: the claimant is the parent living in the UK with parental responsibility, Alex lives with them, Alex is under 16 and in full-time education, Alex is not in extended hospital or care placement, and there are no immigration restrictions.
